response: This code implements the classic producer-consumer concurrency pattern, using a shared thread-safe queue to coordinate work between separate producer and consumer worker threads. Producers add work items to the shared queue, then add a `None` sentinel to signal all work is submitted; consumers pull and pass items to the `process()` work function, and when a consumer hits the `None` sentinel, it requeues the sentinel for other consumers to receive to trigger a coordinated graceful shutdown of all consumers once all work is finished.
